topic: A student learns their scholarship application was denied
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email sat accusingly on the screen, a stark block of text mocking the hours I’d poured into that essay. My hands were clammy as I reread the rejection notice for the prestigious scholarship. A low, tight knot formed in my stomach, a constant, uncomfortable pressure. I kept glancing at the clock, the seconds dragging by with agonizing slowness. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small confines of my dorm room, the worn carpet muffling my footsteps. Every breath felt shallow, catching in my throat. I wanted to smash the laptop, to rip the paper in half, but I just stood there, frozen, the silence of the room amplifying the frantic drumming in my chest.

[story 2]

"Denied." The single word echoed in the quiet hallway, bouncing off the cinderblock walls. I clutched the envelope until my knuckles were white, the cheap paper crinkling under the tension of my grip. My palms were slick, and I could feel a distinct tremor running up my arms. I walked to the window, staring out at the indifferent campus quad. The sunlight felt harsh, exposing the mess inside my head. I kept replaying the interview, searching for a flaw, a moment where I had stumbled, a tiny imperfection that could have derailed everything. My jaw was clenched so tightly I could taste copper.

[story 3]

I stared at my reflection in the darkened monitor. My face looked pale, the shadows under my eyes deep and bruised. A persistent buzzing sensation vibrated just beneath my skin, a restless energy that refused to settle. I picked up my phone, my fingers hovering over the contact list, then letting the phone drop onto the desk with a dull thud. The silence was oppressive; it felt like someone had turned down the volume on the world. I began methodically straightening every book on my shelf, a compulsive need for order battling a surging wave of chaotic anxiety. I kept running my tongue over my dry lips, trying to steady the rapid pulse hammering against my ribs.
